headerTitle: Forward-Deployed AI Data Engineer  ·  Messy Data in Production  ·  Client Embed  ·  AI Agent Deployment
subjectText: Forward-Deployed AI Data Engineer — Edisyl (Remote) — Will Austin
intro: Most of the data I’ve worked with in production was not AI-ready when I got to it. County appraisal district records across inconsistent schemas. Unstructured email archives parsed into CRM routing logic. Supplier invoices with no standard format. The pattern is always the same: the data exists, it’s just not clean. That’s the problem I’ve been building solutions for.
bullets: Production data pipelines on real unstructured data — Python pipeline processing 1.4M+ property records across three Texas county appraisal district APIs, each with different field schemas, inconsistent address formats, and null-heavy ownership records. Built a multi-stage resolution pipeline: normalization, fuzzy entity matching with confidence scoring, human review queue for low-confidence matches, and structured output to a downstream SMS outreach system. The agent never sees ambiguous input — ambiguity routes to review.

Forward-deployed client implementation, four external engagements — each starting with discovering what data actually existed vs. what the client thought existed. Roofing contractor: three county property databases with no consistent schema, CRM with 18 months of untagged leads. Home services: email archive of 400+ inquiry threads parsed and routed into a structured pipeline. OOH advertising: disconnected spreadsheets converted into a live inventory and booking system. Embed, assess the real data environment, build for what exists — not what should exist.

AI agent workflows on imperfect data — 14 live AI endpoints built with the assumption that input data will be wrong some percentage of the time. Zod-validated output contracts on every endpoint so failures surface before they reach users. HITL queues for low-confidence outputs. Structured fallback routing when the model can’t produce a valid result. Strong opinions on why most AI deployments fail: data quality and validation architecture, not model capability.

Python, SQL, REST API integration, full-stack delivery — Python for data pipelines, batch AI processing, and API orchestration; PostgreSQL with complex relational schemas; 100+ REST API routes; IMAP email parsing (imapflow); webhook-driven pipeline triggers; Google OAuth2; Stripe; Twilio and Sinch SMS. Four client engagements delivered from data discovery through production handoff, each with documented playbooks the client operated without technical support.
closingLine: The forward-deployed data problem Edisyl is solving — deploying AI agents against unprepared enterprise data and producing measurable output — is the exact environment these systems were built for. Available immediately. TX-based, remote.
matchNotes: Strong: FDE client embed model exact, Python data pipelines on real messy data (1.4M+ records), AI agent workflows with validation patterns, 4 client implementations with playbook handoffs, SQL production use. Gaps: no DuckDB/dbt, no enterprise warehouse experience (Snowflake/BigQuery), no financial services domain. Requires two written application answers.
